title: Session restart continuity
summary: A repository-local agent restarts across sessions, preserves continuity evidence, and is re-recognized as the same agent rather than treated as a new actor.

stages.title: Active session
stages.summary: The agent is operating normally inside an active repository session with current identity, task, and revision surfaces in place.
stages.meaning: Continuum does not assume a timeless agent. It starts from concrete working state: session, repository, and authored artifacts.

stages.title: Checkpoint recorded
stages.summary: A memory checkpoint is created before handoff or interruption, preserving the local continuity surface.
stages.meaning: The goal is not immortality of one process. The goal is enough preserved evidence that another session can be judged as meaningfully continuous.

stages.title: Restart declared
stages.summary: A migration declaration records that a prior session ended and a new session is attempting to continue the same agent lineage.
stages.meaning: Instead of pretending nothing happened, Continuum records the break and gives later assessment something explicit to evaluate.

stages.title: Assessment refresh
stages.summary: Continuity assessment re-reads the repository bundle and the migration evidence to decide whether the restarted session remains the same agent.
stages.meaning: Continuity in Continuum is judged, not assumed. It depends on recoverable evidence and stable responsibility constraints.

stages.title: Same agent recognized
stages.summary: The restarted session is recognized as the same agent with high confidence and ready recognition status.
stages.meaning: This is why session death does not have to collapse identity. What matters is whether a new session can satisfy public continuity conditions.
